action.reasoning: Observation: Iteration 4 achieved 3.55% average accuracy. Only task 9 has non-zero accuracy (35.5%), confirming catastrophic forgetting and likely a broken replay buffer (previous tasks are exactly 0.0). The custom ResNet-18 can memorize (sanity check passed), but sequential training without effective replay fails completely. Analysis: The previous attempts using a custom ResNet trained end-to-end are too prone to forgetting with tiny buffers, and the buffer code likely had a concatenation bug. Given the compute budget (26 iterations, ~4.7h), I should switch to a much more sample-efficient strategy: use a pre-downloaded pretrained ViT-B/16 as a fixed feature extractor, maintain a class-balanced feature buffer, and train only a lightweight linear head incrementally. This decouples representation learning from classifier learning, making replay extremely effective and training very fast. Decision: Implement a new model.py using timm's pretrained ViT-B/16 (frozen backbone), new train.py that extracts features once per task, stores class-balanced feature exemplars, and trains only the linear head. Run a fast test with 10 epochs per task to validate the pipeline and measure iteration time, then submit eval.
